meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no version control (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no version control. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing & Version Control: Student Developer Checklist
content.seo_description: Master SaaS pricing and fix version control issues with this expert checklist for students. Use Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first real app.
content.answer_block: Without version control, your code is a house of cards. Integrate Git early, then map your SaaS pricing tiers to specific feature flags. This ensures your revenue model is tied to code deployment, allowing you to iterate safely while tracking every change, feature, and financial milestone in your development lifecycle.
content.intro:
You are staring at a folder of 'final_v2_real_fixed.js' files, paralyzed by the fear that one wrong keystroke will erase weeks of progress. This is the 'no version control' trap—the silent killer of student developer ambition. Without a historical record of your logic, you aren't just coding; you are gambling with your own time and intellectual property.

But the pivot from amateur to engineer happens when you stop managing files and start managing systems. By integrating professional version control with a strategic SaaS pricing architecture, you transform your project into a scalable product. This isn't just about saving code; it’s about building a foundation that allows you to charge for the value you create.

steps.title: Initialize Git and Remote Repository
steps.description: Stop working on local files. Initialize a repository to track every iteration and create a safety net for your feature experiments.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a professional Git repository structure with .gitignore templates for Node.js and environment variables.
steps.claude_code_command: git init && git add . && git commit -m 'Initial commit: Project foundation'

steps.title: Define Tiered Pricing Logic
steps.description: Map your features to pricing tiers (Free, Pro, Enterprise) within your codebase to ensure revenue logic is decoupled from UI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a constants file that defines feature flags mapped to pricing tiers: Free, Pro, and Enterprise.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --path ./src/config/pricing.ts --instruction 'Define an enum for plan types and a feature flag mapping object.'

steps.title: Implement Feature Flag Middleware
steps.description: Protect premium features by checking user subscriptions against the pricing tiers defined in your logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a middleware function that checks if the current user plan has access to the requested feature flag.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --path ./src/middleware/auth.ts --instruction 'Insert a check that verifies user.plan against feature.requiredTier'

steps.title: Branching for Pricing Experiments
steps.description: Never test pricing changes on main. Use branches to safely experiment with different subscription models.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a branch strategy for testing new SaaS pricing tiers without affecting production code.

steps.title: Automate Deployment with Version Tags
steps.description: Use semantic versioning to track which pricing iteration is live in your SaaS environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment script that tags the repository with the current version number and deploys to staging.
steps.claude_code_command: git tag -a v1.0.0 -m 'Release with tiered pricing support'

pro_tips: Always use a .env.example file; never commit your actual Stripe keys or database secrets to Git.
Commit messages should follow Conventional Commits: 'feat(billing): add enterprise tier logic' is better than 'fixed stuff'.
Use 'Git LFS' if your project involves large datasets or AI model weights to keep your repo snappy.
Implement a 'Staging' branch to test your pricing logic with mock payment gateways before hitting production.
Keep your pricing logic in a centralized configuration object to make future adjustments a single-line code change.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing logic is live, deploy your app to a provider like Vercel or Railway and perform a 'production smoke test' to ensure your feature flags correctly gate access to premium functionality.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the blueprint to turn your code into a professional SaaS product. Don't let version control anxiety hold your career back any longer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
